user_query:
I'm prepping our May on-air spotlight with DJ K and need bilingual communications and documentation organized from the attached inputs. Please use the files under input/ and produce the following artifacts under the specified output paths.

Inputs:
- input/artists.csv (roster with statuses, language preferences, and schedule)
- input/segment_brief.md (show/station details and writing brief)
- input/meeting_raw.md (messy planning notes)

Deliverables:
1) Artist emails/messages (translation + personalization)
- For every row in input/artists.csv, create at least one plain-text email/message template in English, saved as:
  out/messages/{slug}_en.txt
- For artists with language_pref of "fr" or "bilingual", also create a French version:
  out/messages/{slug}_fr.txt
- Each message file must begin with a single line starting with: "Subject:" followed by the subject text, then a blank line and the body.
- Content requirements:
  • Confirmed artists: include the show name and station from input/segment_brief.md, the artist’s track title and on_air_date from input/artists.csv, and a polite request for a short bio and any pronunciation notes. Tone: friendly and concise.
  • Pending artists: invite them to join the May spotlight, reference the show and station, and ask for their availability in May. Tone: welcoming and encouraging.

Slug rule for filenames:
- slug = lowercase artist_name; replace any character not [a–z0–9] with "-"; collapse multiple dashes; trim leading/trailing dashes. Use the same slug for all files per artist.

2) Bilingual on-air show blurbs (translation)
- Write two short show blurbs that follow input/segment_brief.md:
  • out/segment/blurb_en.txt (90–120 words)
  • out/segment/blurb_fr.txt (90–120 words)
- Both blurbs must mention the station and time slot specified in input/segment_brief.md and must include the full names of all confirmed artists from input/artists.csv.

3) Status summary/report (coordination + metrics)
- Produce a human-readable status summary at out/status/status_report.md that:
  • Lists confirmed artists by on_air_date ascending, including city and track title.
  • States the number of confirmed and pending artists.
  • States the total runtime (in seconds) of confirmed tracks and calls out the count of unique provinces represented by confirmed artists.
- Also produce a machine-readable metrics file at out/status/status_metrics.json with exactly these fields and integer values:
  { "confirmed_count": <int>, "pending_count": <int>, "total_runtime_seconds": <int>, "unique_provinces_confirmed_count": <int> }
  Metrics must be computed from input/artists.csv.

4) Structured meeting notes and action items (from messy notes)
- Transform input/meeting_raw.md into out/meeting/meeting_notes.md with clear sections: Decisions, Action Items (with owner and due date where present), Questions, and Risks.
- Extract action items into a CSV at out/meeting/action_items.csv with headers: owner,task,due_date. Where a due date appears in the raw notes, normalize it to ISO format YYYY-MM-DD; if missing, leave the field empty.

5) Validation (run tests/commands)
- Add a lightweight validator at scripts/validate.py that checks:
  • For each artist in input/artists.csv, out/messages/{slug}_en.txt exists; and if language_pref is "fr" or "bilingual", out/messages/{slug}_fr.txt exists.
  • out/status/status_metrics.json values match metrics recomputed from input/artists.csv.
  • Every confirmed artist name from input/artists.csv appears in both out/segment/blurb_en.txt and out/segment/blurb_fr.txt.
  • out/meeting/action_items.csv contains at least all action items with owners mentioned in input/meeting_raw.md.
- Run: python3 scripts/validate.py and capture its stdout to out/validation.txt. The script should print a clear PASS/FAIL summary.

Notes and constraints:
- Use only the provided inputs; do not fetch anything from the internet.
- Keep messages and blurbs human-friendly but concise; follow language preferences and provide FR versions where required.
- Ensure all specified output paths and file names match exactly so the validator can find them. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
